description: Doctrinal framework mapping for Judicial Interpretive Matrix - CLEP-v2 Enhanced with Deep Constitutional, Socioeconomic & Geopolitical Matrix

due_process.name: Due Process
key_principles: Procedural due process requires notice and hearing
Substantive due process protects fundamental rights
Mathews v. Eldridge balancing test applies
triggers: Deprivation of life, liberty, or property

non_delegation.name: Non-Delegation Doctrine
key_principles: Congress cannot delegate legislative power
Intelligible principle required for delegation
Standards must guide agency discretion
triggers: Lack of policy guidance in statute

separation_of_powers.name: Separation of Powers
key_principles: Powers divided among three branches
No branch may encroach on another
Checks and balances preserved

fourth_amendment.name: Fourth Amendment Privacy
key_principles: Protection against unreasonable searches
Warrant requirement with probable cause
Reasonable expectation of privacy
Special needs exception narrowly construed

equal_protection.name: Equal Protection
key_principles: Similarly situated persons treated similarly
Rational basis for distinctions
Heightened scrutiny for suspect classifications
Class-of-one protection

administrative_law.name: Administrative Law
key_principles: Reasoned decision-making required
Arbitrary and capricious standard
Substantial evidence for findings
Notice and comment for rulemaking
Independent judicial review
triggers: Departure from prior practice without justification
administrative_law.note: Post-Loper Bright (2024): Courts exercise independent judgment on statutory interpretation without agency deference

unconstitutional_conditions.name: Unconstitutional Conditions Doctrine
key_principles: Government cannot condition benefits on surrender of rights
Coercion of constitutional rights prohibited
Voluntary acceptance must be genuine
triggers: Benefit conditioned on waiver of rights

constitutional_torts.name: Constitutional Torts and Qualified Immunity
key_principles: Government officials liable for constitutional violations
Qualified immunity protects unless clearly established law violated
Objective reasonableness standard for official conduct
Remedy for deprivation of federal rights under color of law

property_rights.name: Property Rights and Economic Liberty
key_principles: Property includes tangible and intangible interests
Government takings require just compensation
Substantive due process protects against arbitrary deprivation
Liberty interests include contractual freedom
triggers: Arbitrary interference with property or contracts

free_movement.name: Right to Travel and Freedom of Movement
key_principles: Right to interstate travel is fundamental
Durational residency requirements require compelling interest
New residents entitled to equal treatment
Identity control measures must not burden fundamental rights
triggers: Differential treatment based on residency status

first_amendment.name: First Amendment Freedoms
key_principles: Content-based restrictions presumptively invalid
Prior restraints on speech disfavored
Public forum doctrine protects expression in traditional public spaces
Free exercise requires neutrality and general applicability
Establishment Clause prohibits religious coercion

second_amendment.name: Second Amendment Right to Keep and Bear Arms
key_principles: Individual right to possess firearms for lawful purposes
Core right of self-defense in the home
Strict scrutiny applies to regulations burdening core right
Presumptively lawful regulations include felons, sensitive places, dangerous weapons

eighth_amendment.name: Eighth Amendment Protections
key_principles: Excessive fines prohibited
Proportionality required for civil and criminal penalties
Cruel and unusual punishment forbidden
Evolving standards of decency govern

federal_power.name: Federal Power and Federalism
key_principles: Enumerated powers limit federal authority
Commerce power extends to activities substantially affecting interstate commerce
Federal law supreme over conflicting state law
Anti-commandeering prevents federal coercion of states

constitutional_structure.name: Constitutional Structure and Foundational Principles
key_principles: Judicial review established by Marbury v. Madison
Federal system divides power between nation and states
Bill of Rights incorporated against states through Fourteenth Amendment
Constitutional text supreme over all other law
triggers: Conflict between constitutional text and government action

economic_regulation.name: Economic Regulation and Commerce
key_principles: Rational basis review for economic regulations
State police power permits health, safety, welfare regulations
Substantial impairment of contracts subject to scrutiny
Price controls permissible if reasonable

police_pretext_doctrine.name: Police Pretext Doctrine
key_principles: Pretextual stops permissible if objective probable cause exists
Subjective intent of officer irrelevant to Fourth Amendment analysis
Traffic violations provide lawful basis for stop regardless of ulterior motive
Pattern evidence may show discriminatory enforcement
triggers: Minor traffic violation used as pretext
Statistical evidence of discriminatory stop patterns
Duration of stop extended beyond traffic mission

metadata_exposure_doctrine.name: Metadata Exposure Doctrine
key_principles: CSLI and location data protected by Fourth Amendment
Metadata collection may constitute search
Third-party doctrine limited for digital data
Warrant generally required for historical location data

administrative_delegation_drift.name: Administrative Delegation Drift
key_principles: Gradual expansion of agency authority without clear congressional authorization
Major questions doctrine limits transformative agency actions
Intelligible principle must guide discretion
Clear statement required for significant policy changes
triggers: Agency claims authority for unprecedented action

digital_trespass_theory.name: Digital Trespass Theory (Post-Carpenter)
key_principles: Digital information may have reasonable expectation of privacy
Physical trespass not required for Fourth Amendment violation
Katz expectation of privacy test applies to digital realm
Carpenter limits third-party doctrine for digital records

procedural_fairness_erosion_index.name: Procedural Fairness Erosion Index
key_principles: Notice and opportunity to be heard required
Mathews balancing test weighs private interest, error risk, government burden
Erosion of procedural protections triggers scrutiny
Post-deprivation remedies sometimes insufficient
triggers: Denial of opportunity to present evidence

economic_sovereignty_doctrine.name: Economic Sovereignty Doctrine
key_principles: States may not discriminate against interstate commerce
Economic protectionism forbidden
Market participant exception permits state favoritism when state acts as buyer/seller
Undue burden on interstate commerce unconstitutional
triggers: State law discriminates against out-of-state goods or services
Differential treatment of residents vs non-residents

coercive_municipal_revenue_models.name: Coercive Municipal Revenue Models
key_principles: Excessive fines violate Eighth Amendment
Revenue-driven enforcement may violate due process
Ability-to-pay considerations required for incarceration
Proportionality required for fines and fees
triggers: Inability to pay leading to incarceration
Civil forfeiture programs targeting property value

surveillance_technology_doctrine.name: Surveillance Technology Doctrine
key_principles: Sense-enhancing technology requires warrant
Aerial surveillance from navigable airspace generally permissible
Biometric collection from arrestees constitutional
Emerging technology subject to Fourth Amendment analysis

traffic_enforcement_doctrine.name: Traffic Enforcement and Mobility Rights
key_principles: Traffic stops require reasonable suspicion
Stop duration limited to traffic mission
Pretextual stops permissible with probable cause
Checkpoints require programmatic necessity

prosecutorial_integrity_doctrine.name: Prosecutorial Integrity and Evidence Disclosure
key_principles: Prosecution must disclose material exculpatory evidence
Knowing use of false testimony violates due process
Suppression of evidence material to guilt or punishment unconstitutional
Disclosure required even if defendant doesn't request

civil_rights_enforcement.name: Civil Rights and Anti-Discrimination
key_principles: Strict scrutiny for racial classifications
Intermediate scrutiny for gender classifications
Rational basis for other classifications
Disparate impact may show discrimination
triggers: Facially neutral law with discriminatory effect

voting_rights_doctrine.name: Voting Rights and Electoral Access
key_principles: Right to vote is fundamental
One person, one vote principle
Racial discrimination in voting prohibited
Strict scrutiny for severe burdens on voting

takings_and_exactions.name: Regulatory Takings and Exactions
key_principles: Physical occupation is categorical taking
Total economic deprivation is taking unless background principle
Permit conditions require essential nexus and rough proportionality
Regulatory takings analyzed under Penn Central factors

criminal_procedure_doctrine.name: Criminal Procedure and Fair Trial Rights
key_principles: Right to counsel in criminal prosecutions
Protection against self-incrimination
Impartial jury trial
Confrontation of witnesses

habeas_corpus_doctrine.name: Habeas Corpus and Detention
key_principles: Habeas corpus suspendable only in rebellion or invasion
Detainees entitled to meaningful hearing
Notice of factual basis required
Opportunity to rebut evidence

excessive_force_doctrine.name: Excessive Force and Police Use of Force
key_principles: Objective reasonableness from officer perspective
Severity of crime, immediate threat, active resistance considered
Deadly force requires imminent threat
Qualified immunity unless clearly established law violated

school_search_doctrine.name: School Searches and Student Rights
key_principles: Reasonable suspicion standard for school searches
Students have reduced privacy expectations
Drug testing permissible for athletes and extracurricular participants
Strip searches require specific justification

checkpoint_doctrine.name: Checkpoint and Suspicionless Search Doctrine
key_principles: Special needs exception permits suspicionless searches
Primary purpose cannot be general crime detection
Administrative or safety purpose required
Programmatic necessity and minimal discretion

biometric_privacy_doctrine.name: Biometric Data and Privacy
key_principles: DNA collection from arrestees constitutional
Facial recognition raises privacy concerns
Fingerprinting standard for booking
Biometric databases subject to Fourth Amendment limits

qualified_immunity_doctrine.name: Qualified Immunity
key_principles: Officials immune unless clearly established law violated
Objective reasonableness standard
Particularized prior case law required
Constitutional right must be clearly established at time of conduct

right_to_jury_doctrine.name: Jury Trial and Jury Selection
key_principles: Right to impartial jury in criminal prosecutions
Jury must represent fair cross-section of community
Peremptory strikes cannot be based on race or gender
Systematic exclusion of groups unconstitutional

sentencing_proportionality.name: Sentencing and Proportionality
key_principles: Grossly disproportionate sentences violate Eighth Amendment
Death penalty for juveniles unconstitutional
Mandatory LWOP for juveniles unconstitutional
Intellectual disability bars execution

interstate_commerce_doctrine.name: Interstate Commerce and Dormant Commerce Clause
key_principles: States cannot discriminate against interstate commerce
Pike balancing for non-discriminatory burdens
Market participant exception
Congressional authorization permits state regulation

preemption_doctrine.name: Federal Preemption
key_principles: Federal law supreme over conflicting state law
Express, implied, and field preemption
Conflict preemption when compliance impossible
Obstacle preemption when state law frustrates federal objectives
triggers: State law conflicts with federal statute

substantive_due_process.name: Substantive Due Process
key_principles: Protects fundamental rights from arbitrary government action
Shocks-the-conscience standard for executive action
Strict scrutiny for infringement of fundamental rights
Liberty includes intimate decisions

affirmative_action_doctrine.name: Affirmative Action and Race-Conscious Programs
key_principles: Race-conscious admissions now unconstitutional (SFFA 2023)
Strict scrutiny applies to all racial classifications
Diversity no longer compelling interest for admissions
Colorblind approach required

political_question_doctrine.name: Political Question and Justiciability
key_principles: Certain questions committed to political branches
Partisan gerrymandering non-justiciable
Impeachment is political question
Lack of judicially manageable standards

establishment_clause_doctrine.name: Establishment Clause
key_principles: Government cannot establish religion
No coercion of religious belief or practice
Historical practices may be constitutional
Neutrality toward religion required

free_exercise_doctrine.name: Free Exercise of Religion
key_principles: Neutral laws of general applicability need not accommodate religion
Targeted religious discrimination triggers strict scrutiny
Religious exemptions not required from neutral laws
Substantial burden on religious exercise requires justification

major_questions_doctrine.name: Major Questions Doctrine
major_questions_doctrine.description: Agency must have clear congressional authorization for decisions of vast economic and political significance

clear_statement_rule.name: Clear Statement Rule
clear_statement_rule.description: Congress must clearly state intent when affecting fundamental rights or federal-state balance
clear_statement_rule.standard: Plain statement of congressional intent required

chevron_deference.name: Chevron Deference (Overruled 2024)
chevron_deference.description: Courts defer to reasonable agency interpretation of ambiguous statute
chevron_deference.status: OVERRULED by Loper Bright Enterprises v. Raimondo (2024)
chevron_deference.replacement: Courts now exercise independent judgment on all statutory questions
chevron_deference.historical_note: Prior to 2024, Chevron provided two-step framework: (1) Is statute ambiguous? (2) Is agency interpretation reasonable?

auer_deference.name: Auer/Seminole Rock Deference
auer_deference.description: Agency interpretation of own regulation entitled to deference unless plainly erroneous
auer_deference.note: Subject to limitations from Kisor v. Wilkie (2019)

rule_of_lenity.name: Rule of Lenity
rule_of_lenity.description: Ambiguous criminal statutes construed in favor of defendant
rule_of_lenity.standard: Reasonable doubt resolved in favor of defendant

constitutional_avoidance.name: Constitutional Avoidance Canon
constitutional_avoidance.description: Interpret statutes to avoid constitutional questions

digital_privacy_protection.name: Digital Privacy Protection Framework
digital_privacy_protection.description: Modern Fourth Amendment protection for digital information and electronic communications
applicable_when: Cell phone or digital device searches
digital_privacy_protection.standard: Warrant generally required absent exigent circumstances or valid exception

chain_of_custody_integrity.name: Chain of Custody and Evidence Integrity
chain_of_custody_integrity.description: Fruit of poisonous tree doctrine and evidentiary integrity requirements
applicable_when: Evidence derived from illegal search or seizure
chain_of_custody_integrity.standard: Evidence inadmissible if fruit of constitutional violation unless independent source or attenuation

police_accountability.name: Police Accountability and Use of Force
police_accountability.description: Fourth Amendment reasonableness standard for use of force and qualified immunity limits
police_accountability.standard: Objective reasonableness from perspective of officer on scene; qualified immunity unless clearly established law violated

rational_basis_with_bite.name: Rational Basis with Bite
rational_basis_with_bite.description: Enhanced rational basis review when classification based on animus
applicable_when: Classification based on animus or prejudice
rational_basis_with_bite.standard: Government must show rational relationship to legitimate interest; animus invalidates

strict_scrutiny.name: Strict Scrutiny
strict_scrutiny.description: Highest level of judicial review for fundamental rights and suspect classifications
strict_scrutiny.standard: Narrowly tailored to compelling government interest

intermediate_scrutiny.name: Intermediate Scrutiny
intermediate_scrutiny.standard: Substantially related to important government interest
